flags.concern: Interaction state plumbing: step 1 says the new interaction policy should be readable by `VideoEditorShell`, `TimelineEditor`, `TimelineCanvas`, `OverlayEditor`, and `PropertiesPanel`, but the plan only names `useTimelineState.ts`, `useTimelineState.types.ts`, and a new helper. In this repository those consumers read the split editor contexts built in `VideoEditorProvider.tsx`, so the shared policy will not actually reach most of the named surfaces unless the provider/context layer is part of the implementation.
flags.evidence: Checked /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx, /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx, and /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/PropertiesPanel.tsx; they consume `useTimelineEditorData()` / `useTimelineEditorOps()`. Those values are explicitly selected and re-exposed in /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.tsx rather than reading `useTimelineState()` directly.

flags.concern: Input modality routing: the plan distinguishes `iPad touch` from `iPad keyboard/trackpad`, but the proposed state model only adds `deviceClass` and mode flags. The live interaction code routes drag, scrub, and resize through generic pointer handlers, so following the plan literally would still apply tablet touch rules to mouse/trackpad input on the same device class and risk regressing the current desktop-style pointer behavior.
flags.evidence: Checked /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts, which listens to document-level `pointerdown`; /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimeRuler.tsx and /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx, which handle scrub/resize through pointer events; and /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/OverlayEditor.tsx, which still has separate mouse handlers. The plan never specifies a per-event `pointerType` or equivalent modality gate.

flags.concern: Shot-group touch actions: the plan replaces right-click-only access in `ClipAction.tsx`, but it leaves the separate shot-group action surface out of scope. Shot-group navigation, unpin/delete, and final-video switching currently live behind `onContextMenu` on hover-revealed labels plus very small badge buttons, so touch users would still lack a reliable way to access those timeline actions.
flags.evidence: Checked /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx and /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ShotGroupContextMenu.tsx. Shot-group labels use `opacity-0` / `hover:opacity-100` and open their menu only from `onContextMenu`, while the final-video/stale badges are rendered as 16px controls.
